source.title: The EPED Pedestal Model: Extensions, Experimental Tests, and Application to ELM-suppressed Regimes
source.author: P. B. Snyder, M. Beurskens, R. J. Groebner, J. W. Hughes, R. Nazikian, D. Orlov, T. H. Osborne, O. Schmitz, M. R. Wade, J. Candy, L. Frassinetti, J. R. Walk, H. R. Wilson, X. Q. Xu
source.venue: APS Division of Plasma Physics annual meeting presentation (General Atomics public preprint; title page states 53rd APS-DPP, Chicago, 8-12 November 2010; slide footers state PB Snyder/APS-DPP/2011)
source.verification: PDF fetched and slides read in-session; values digitised visually from the named slides
citations: Snyder P. B. et al., Nucl. Fusion 51, 103016 (2011)
Snyder P. B. et al., Phys. Plasmas 16, 056118 (2009)
Snyder P. B. et al., Phys. Plasmas 9, 2037 (2002)

digitisation_uncertainty.note: Values read visually from figure markers; treat comparisons within these bands as indistinguishable.

validation_statistics.slide: 2011: DIII-D Upgrade to Thomson System Allows More Precise Height & Width Comparison

diiid_ip_scan.slide: Interaction of P-B and KBM Constraints Predicts Pedestal Height and Width Changes in Ip Scan

machine_inputs.note: Only Bt, kappa, delta and the Ip values are stated on the slide. R0, a, and n_ped are NOT published there; any benchmark against these rows must declare its assumed geometry and scan the density.
